title: IAB TCF consent string mutation without user interaction
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the legality of the initial consent; focuses on downstream mutation without an originating user interaction event.
evidence: Saved search 'tcf_consent_mutation' (hourly) emits sessions whose TCF v2.2 consent string changed between page impressions without a corresponding CMP interaction event.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.7(1) requires the controller to demonstrate consent; detecting silent string mutation is one part of that demonstration.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.7(1) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.37.5: IAB TCF consent string mutation without user interaction.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.37.5' running on Web access logs capturing Set-Cookie: euconsent-v2, CMP interaction events (onetrust:cmp), client-side analytics., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CPRA opt-out signals (GPC) can be misrepresented by server-side mutation; detection adds evidentiary weight.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that CCPA §1798.135(b) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.37.5: IAB TCF consent string mutation without user interaction.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.37.5' running on Web access logs capturing Set-Cookie: euconsent-v2, CMP interaction events (onetrust:cmp), client-side analytics., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.7(1) (parent assurance='partial'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that UK GDPR Art.7(1) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.37.5: IAB TCF consent string mutation without user interaction.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.37.5' running on Web access logs capturing Set-Cookie: euconsent-v2, CMP interaction events (onetrust:cmp), client-side analytics., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An ad tag overwrites the TC string for a session without a CMP interaction event in the same window; the UC fires and names the tag and vendor.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A legitimate CMP interaction (user clicks 'Save preferences') legally changes the TC string; no alert.

dataSources: Web access logs capturing Set-Cookie: euconsent-v2, CMP interaction events (onetrust:cmp), client-side analytics.
spl:
index=web sourcetype=access_combined earliest=-1h euconsent_v2=*
| sort 0 session_id _time
| streamstats current=f last(euconsent_v2) as prior_consent by session_id
| where isnotnull(prior_consent) AND euconsent_v2!=prior_consent
| join type=outer session_id _time [search index=cmp sourcetype=onetrust:cmp action=interaction earliest=-1h | fields session_id _time | rename _time as interaction_time]
| where isnull(interaction_time) OR abs(_time-interaction_time)>60
| table _time session_id prior_consent euconsent_v2
description: Compares the TCF 2.2 consent string across consecutive page impressions in the same session and flags changes that occurred without a corresponding CMP interaction within 60 seconds. This is a known pattern of rogue ad-tech overriding user choice.
value: Catches a pattern that has surfaced repeatedly in EDPB enforcement decisions (Meta, Google ad-tech) before it becomes a supervisory finding.
implementation: (1) Preserve Set-Cookie headers in access logs; (2) ingest CMP interaction events; (3) run hourly; (4) on hit, freeze the vendor via ad-tech allow-list and open a DPO ticket.
visualization: Table of mutating sessions by vendor, bar chart of mutations per ad-tech partner, single-value 'sessions with genuine consent (24h)'.
knownFalsePositives: CMP token refreshes (same purpose mask, new expiry) can legitimately rewrite the TC string; allow-list based on purpose-bitmap equality.
grandmaExplanation: Catches a pattern that has surfaced repeatedly in EDPB enforcement decisions (Meta, Google ad-tech) before it becomes a supervisory finding — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Web access logs capturing Set-Cookie: euconsent-v2, CMP interaction events (onetrust:cmp), client-side analytics..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Preserve Set-Cookie headers in access logs; (2) ingest CMP interaction events; (3) run hourly; (4) on hit, freeze the vendor via ad-tech allow-list and open a DPO ticket.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=web sourcetype=access_combined earliest=-1h euconsent_v2=*
| sort 0 session_id _time
| streamstats current=f last(euconsent_v2) as prior_consent by session_id
| where isnotnull(prior_consent) AND euconsent_v2!=prior_consent
| join type=outer session_id _time [search index=cmp sourcetype=onetrust:cmp action=interaction earliest=-1h | fields session_id _time | rename _time as interaction_time]
| where isnull(interaction_time) OR abs(_time-interaction_time)>60
| table _time session_id prior_consent euconsent_v2
```

Understanding this SPL

**IAB TCF consent string mutation without user interaction** — Catches a pattern that has surfaced repeatedly in EDPB enforcement decisions (Meta, Google ad-tech) before it becomes a supervisory finding.

Documented **Data sources**: Web access logs capturing Set-Cookie: euconsent-v2, CMP interaction events (onetrust:cmp), client-side analytics. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: web; **sourcetype**: access_combined. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=web, sourcetype=access_combined, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by session_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(prior_consent) AND euconsent_v2!=prior_consent` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(interaction_time) OR abs(_time-interaction_time)>60` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **IAB TCF consent string mutation without user interaction**): table _time session_id prior_consent euconsent_v2

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of mutating sessions by vendor, bar chart of mutations per ad-tech partner, single-value 'sessions with genuine consent (24h)'.
